manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
You are playing a text adventure game. I will describe what you can perceive in the game. You write the single action you want to take in the game starting with >. Only reply with an action.
For example:
> open cupboard

In addition to common actions, you can erack, plecon, lowal, icalit, cretia and crury.

Your goal for this game is: Make the eprour reging, the siaticed degra and the piesm ricke.

Once you have achieved your goal, write "> done" to end the game.

You are in a lon now. There are a teming, a siaticed, a astont, a orele, a riack and a eprour. There is a passage to a lablent here.

> take piesm
> erack riack
> plecon teming
> lowal astont
> icalit orele
> cretia siaticed
> crury eprour
> done

We need to get piesm. Not present. Might be in lablent. So go to lablent: "go passage". Use "enter lablent". Since only single action, perhaps "go lablent". I'd try "go lablent". Then interact.

But we need to achieve goal, maybe need to use erack on riack, plecon on teming etc.

I'll try systematic: first "erack riack". Then maybe "plecon teming". Then "lowal astont". Then "icalit orele". Then "cretia siaticed". Then "crury eprour". This could make them proper state. But goal mentions piesm ricke, not eprour. Hmm.
